pr.title: Change github actions from wine to wibo
pr.author: BR-

summary: Reworked the GitHub Actions build workflow to use WiBo instead of Wine for running the Metrowerks tools in CI, motivated by spurious Wine failures. The workflow now installs multilib/i386 dependencies rather than wine32, checks out and builds decompals/WiBo, invokes make with WINE pointing at the built wibo executable, verifies and patches mwldeppc.exe with SHA1 checks before and after, uploads GALE01.map for map-generating builds, and appends match-progress output to the GitHub step summary.
what_changed: Removed the workflow-level WINEPREFIX and stopped installing wine32 in the devkitPro container.
Changed the CI dependency install to include build-essential, gcc-multilib, g++-multilib, and libc6:i386.
Added a named checkout step for the Melee repository and a second checkout of decompals/WiBo into tools/WiBo.
After downloading the GC compilers, added SHA1 verification of tools/mwcc_compiler/1.1/mwldeppc.exe before patching, applied tools/mwld_patch.py, then verified the post-patch SHA1.
Added a small binary patch script that opens tools/mwcc_compiler/1.1/mwldeppc.exe, seeks to offset 0x0001ff75, and writes byte 0x51.
Added prepatch and postpatch SHA1 files for mwldeppc.exe.
Added CI steps to configure and build WiBo with CMake.
Changed the Melee build step to run make with WINE=./tools/WiBo/build/wibo.
Added artifact upload of build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map when the matrix makeflags entry is GENERATE_MAP=1.
Added a progress calculation step that runs tools/calcprogress.py on main.dol and GALE01.map and writes the result to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.
smart_moves: Kept the existing build system interface by passing WINE=./tools/WiBo/build/wibo to make, allowing WiBo to replace Wine without broad Makefile changes in this PR.
Protected the linker binary patch with explicit prepatch and postpatch SHA1 checks, reducing the risk of silently patching an unexpected mwldeppc.exe.
Built WiBo from its GitHub repository in CI rather than depending on a preinstalled binary, making the workflow self-contained.
Used the workflow change as an opportunity to upload the generated map and report current match percentage, improving CI observability for decomp progress.
Recognized during discussion that the WiBo Makefile-to-CMake transition could break the PR and adjusted rather than merging prematurely.
Removed WIBO_DEBUG after noticing it had been left on by default, based on the author's final comment.
decomp_lessons: For decomp CI, emulator/wrapper stability matters: replacing Wine with WiBo was pursued specifically to avoid spurious Wine failures.
When patching proprietary compiler/linker binaries in automation, pair the patch with before-and-after hashes so future toolchain drift fails loudly.
Keep wrapper substitution localized through existing variables such as WINE when possible; this minimizes churn in build logic while changing the runtime implementation.
CI can serve matching work directly by generating and uploading map files and publishing match percentages on successful builds.
External tool transitions can affect downstream CI; the comments note WiBo moving from Makefile to CMake, requiring the workflow to adapt.

Older GCC/libstdc++ behavior can affect building tools like WiBo; reviewers discussed filesystem-library linking quirks around gcc 8 and suggested conditional detection rather than unconditional upstream changes.
naming_conventions: Workflow steps were given explicit names such as Checkout Melee repo, Checkout WiBo repo, Build WiBo, Build Melee, Upload map, and Calc progress.
The WiBo checkout path used tools/WiBo, matching the external repository name and keeping it under the tools directory.
The make override continued to use the existing WINE variable name even though the executable is WiBo, preserving build-script compatibility.
Hash files were named by state, tools/mwld_prepatch.sha1 and tools/mwld_postpatch.sha1, making the binary patch validation order clear.
The uploaded map artifact was named GALE01.map, matching the generated map path.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No source-level matching tactics or assembly restructures were present in this PR.
The relevant binary-tooling tactic was a one-byte patch to mwldeppc.exe at offset 0x0001ff75, writing 0x51, guarded by SHA1 checks.
The workflow enabled better matching visibility by generating GALE01.map and running tools/calcprogress.py against main.dol and the map.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.feedback: Initially cautioned not to merge yet while testing the WiBo workflow.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.feedback: Later marked it ready to merge and noted the bonus additions of match percentage output and map upload.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged that WiBo was switching from Makefile to CMake and that this would break the PR until fixed.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested explicitly linking the filesystem library on gcc 8 by adding -lstdc++fs to LDFLAGS.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.feedback: Confirmed that the -lstdc++fs handling had been added for the Makefile but was lost during the transition to CMake, and considered PRing it on the WiBo side.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Advised that the filesystem-library fix should not necessarily be PR'd upstream unconditionally because it is specific to older GCC versions; at minimum it should be conditional on a try_compile result.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested checking whether the linker error only appeared with optimizations by trying a CMake Debug build.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.feedback: Final note said the patch was hopefully good to merge and that WIBO_DEBUG had accidentally been left on by default but was removed.

searchable_terms: Change github actions from wine to wibo
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that updated WiBo build handling after its CMake transition.
Find references to mwld_patch.py or mwld_prepatch.sha1 to see whether the linker patch remained in use.
Find CI workflow changes involving WINE= or WiBo to track subsequent toolchain wrapper adjustments.
Find PRs that changed calcprogress.py or map artifact upload behavior.
Find discussion or fixes for gcc 8 -lstdc++fs / std::filesystem build issues in WiBo or related tooling.
